The hand taps through various screens, starting with a music app displaying a song titled 'Bad girl' by Margo - delight, complete with a play button, progress bar, and volume controls. The hand then plugs in a pair of real black wired earbuds, cuts the wire with scissors, and simulates playback by tapping the screen. The hand proceeds to navigate through the phone’s interface, swiping to a home screen with hand-drawn app icons, then opening the Maps app to display a hand-drawn map of India. The hand continues to swipe through the phone’s library and games, revealing a grid of colorful, hand-drawn game icons including 'Mario' and 'Roblox'. Throughout, the hand performs precise gestures like tapping, swiping, and pinching, all on the static cardboard device, creating an illusion of a functional smartphone. The background remains a consistent light wooden surface, and the time displayed on the phone is consistently 9:42. The video maintains a playful, creative tone, emphasizing the craftsmanship of the hand-drawn elements and the realistic simulation of phone interactions.

segments.caption: The video showcases a hand interacting with a handmade cardboard smartphone, designed to mimic a foldable device like the Samsung Galaxy Fold. The device has a black cardboard body with a white screen area where hand-drawn interfaces are displayed. Initially, the screen shows a camera app with a cute Shiba Inu dog illustration, which the hand taps to 'take a photo' by flipping the screen. The hand then unfolds the device to reveal a larger screen, simulating a tablet mode, displaying a hand-drawn boy with a red shirt and blue pants. The hand swipes to reveal a weather screen with a sun and 13°, then navigates to a home screen filled with colorful app icons, including Play Store, YouTube, Maps, Google, Chrome, Gmail, and others. The hand taps the 'Cut the Rope' app, which opens to a game screen with a green monster and a candy hanging from a rope. The hand simulates gameplay by pulling the rope, causing the candy to fall and the monster to eat it, with stars and a score appearing. The hand then returns to the home screen, swipes to the weather app again, and taps the screen to simulate voice command activation with the text 'Say 'OK Google'' visible. The entire sequence is performed on a clean white background with consistent lighting, emphasizing the hand-drawn, playful, and interactive nature of the cardboard device.
